{{Quote|Base, this is TK8252. We have eliminated the intruder. Repeat, we have eliminated the intruder. Out.|TK8252, after Kyle Katarn falls down the chasm|<ref name="MotS" />}}
'''TK8252''' was the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] of a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[field stormtrooper]] in service with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], TK8252 was stationed aboard one of the two artificial [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] that [[Battle of Altyr V (Galactic Civil War)|attacked]] a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Altyr V base|base]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Altyr V]]. When [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Kyle Katarn]] infiltrated TK8252's asteroid in an attempt to sabotage it, TK8252 and another [[Stormtrooper/Legends|trooper]] tried to prevent him from doing so by attacking him with [[rail detonator]]s. In the end, however, Katarn successfully destroyed both asteroids.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Blast him!|TK8252, upon spotting Katarn|<ref name="MotS" />}}
TK8252 was the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] of a [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="MotS" /> [[field stormtrooper]], the highest rank of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]],<ref name="Manual">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' game manual</ref> serving the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. By [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], TK8252 was serving aboard one of the two artificial [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] created by the Empire to be used as mobile military bases.<ref name="MotS" /> During [[Operation Shadow Hand]],<ref name="NEGTC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> the two asteroids [[Battle of Altyr V (Galactic Civil War)|attacked]] a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Altyr V base|outpost]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Altyr V]]. However, [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Kyle Katarn]] was on Altyr V during the attack, and he tried to sabotage the asteroids. Using a stolen [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|Imperial shuttle]], Katarn infiltrated the asteroid where TK8252 was stationed. As Katarn made his way through the asteroid, TK8252 and another field stormtrooper spotted him and pursued him through one of the asteroid's corridors. Using [[rail detonator]]s, they fired [[rail charge]]s at Katarn, one of which exploded near the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], causing him to fall into a chasm. Believing that it was impossible for Katarn to survive such a fall, TK8252 reported to the base that the intruder had been [[Death/Legends|eliminated]]. Katarn did survive, however, and he used the asteroid's weapons to destroy the other asteroid and then set TK8252's asteroid on self-destruct, escaping in the last moment before its explosion.<ref name="MotS" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|There's no way he survived that fall.|TK8252, on Kyle Katarn|<ref name="MotS" />}}
During the events at Altyr V, TK8252 underestimated Katarn, believing that the Jedi would not survive the fall down the chasm.<ref name="MotS" />

==Behind the scenes==
TK8252 appeared in a single cutscene in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Mysteries of the Sith]]'',<ref name="MotS" /> where he was voiced by [[Kevin Schmitt]], the game's lead level designer.<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|RyanKaufman|42|Insider 89 "Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum & Villainy" Endnotes}}</ref> The game does not specify which one of the two stormtroopers pursuing Katarn is TK8252.<ref name="MotS" /> In [[2006]], one of the game's developers, [[Ryan Kaufman (real person)|Ryan Kaufman]], revealed in his blog that the stormtrooper was named after Schmitt's telephone extension number at the time. However, in his blog, Kaufman calls the character TK-825, missing the two at the end and adding a hyphen,<ref name="Blog" /> while in the game's subtitles, there is no hyphen in the call sign.<ref name="MotS" />
